title: pinguicula vulgaris subsp. macroceras
intro: Pinguicula vulgaris subsp. macroceras, also known as the large-flowered butterwort, is a carnivorous plant species belonging to the family Lentibulariaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Pinguicula vulgaris subsp. macroceras include its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation, making it a potential remedy for skin conditions and other inflammatory-related issues. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing its leaves in water or using it as a tincture, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human use.
